location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Tuesdays, Thursdays, and a third flex day); up to 4 weeks remote per year

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in analytics, strategy, consulting, investment banking, product analytics, or business operations
Individual contributor role explicitly stated as 'highly visible, high-impact individual contributor role'
Comp range $185,500–$255,000 consistent with senior/staff IC level
Expected to mentor analysts and influence org-level practices without direct management

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in analytics, strategy, consulting, investment banking, product analytics, or business operations at a high‑growth technology or marketplace company.
Proven ability to own ambiguous, high‑stakes analytical problems end‑to‑end and drive them to clear business impact.
Deep expertise in marketing analytics, with experience in lifecycle/CRM measurement, experimentation, and ROI analysis.
Strong understanding of attribution methodologies and causal inference tradeoffs in a marketing context.
Exceptional analytical rigor — able to define metrics, size opportunities, and execute complex analyses independently.
Advanced SQL proficiency; comfort working directly with large, imperfect datasets.
Excellent communication and storytelling skills, with experience influencing senior leaders through data.
Strong business and product intuition, with the ability to connect analyses to broader company goals.
Ability to lead through influence, operate autonomously, and thrive in a fast‑paced, evolving environment.

scope.surface: Lifecycle & CRM marketing analytics — email, push, onsite channels across a two-sided marketplace (retailers and brands)
core_responsibilities: Own the end‑to‑end measurement framework for Lifecycle & CRM marketing globally
Act as the primary analytics thought partner to Lifecycle Marketing, Strategic Finance, and Marketing Leadership
Define and evolve north‑star KPIs and success metrics for lifecycle marketing
Set the standard for experimentation strategy within Lifecycle/CRM
Design, analyze, and synthesize experiments focused on driving user behavior
Be the subject‑matter expert on marketing attribution for owned channels
Own and evolve payback, ROI, and LTV frameworks for lifecycle marketing
Lead analyses to assess and improve attribution methodologies
Surface proactive insights by deeply understanding lifecycle performance trends
Build and own strategic dashboards and reporting
Influence analytical best practices across Marketing Analytics
Mentor and level up analysts through informal coaching

green_flags: High-visibility individual contributor role with genuine strategic ownership
Clear articulation of cross-functional partners and scope
Hybrid policy clearly defined (3 days/week in-office with 4-week remote flexibility)
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for non-US candidates
San Francisco and New York City only — no remote option; fully remote candidates ineligible
Role is explicitly an individual contributor but expected to mentor analysts and influence org-level practices — scope may be expansive for a non-management role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, years required, and IC description)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as two-sided wholesale marketplace)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed explicitly in JD)
ambiguities: Salary range is stated only for San Francisco, CA and New York City, NY — it is unclear whether other locations are eligible or whether pay differs for non-listed cities.
The JD lists no preferred/nice-to-have qualifications separately; all qualifications are presented as required, so nice_to_haves is empty.
